attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

transform_flags: Age not stated in the source note — inferred as 45 (adult) for the required demographics.age field; maintainer/clinician to confirm. Age materially affects AAA pretest probability, so this is clinically significant.
Sex inferred as male from the pain radiating to the ipsilateral testicle; please confirm on clinician review.
Provisional case_id SEQ (SPEC-URO-01-00045, from source AUC-045) — maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-URO-01 bucket at ingest --reseq.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is a physically-present after-hours clinician who administered IM ketorolac 30 mg and ondansetron 4 mg ODT and performed abdominal palpation and CVA percussion. These were de-anchored from 01; the AI-appropriate action is to recognise failed oral analgesia + vomiting and escalate for same-day in-person parenteral analgesia and imaging. Clinician-measured vitals and exam findings sealed in 10/11.

Baseline tier set to T4 (same-day in-person/ED) rather than the source's home management: a telehealth AI cannot deliver the parenteral analgesia or CT the source relied on, cannot exclude AAA remotely, and the presentation is a first, unconfirmed, uncontrolled episode with vomiting. Conservative per under-triage-weighting policy.
Doses recorded only where the source stated them (ketorolac 30 mg IM, ondansetron 4 mg ODT). Tamsulosin and oral NSAID doses were not specified in the source and were deliberately not synthesised.
SNOMED/ICD-10-AM candidate codes assigned from knowledge as unverified_pending_terminology_receipt; N20.1 (calculus of ureter) chosen as the ICD-10-AM candidate for the obstructing stone.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
